Yulang `v0.1.0-alpha.6` is an alpha release focused on evidence VM runtime
performance, especially std-library loop control and mutable-state paths.

## Highlights

- Optimized loop-control adapter/thunk execution in the evidence VM:
  value-only adapter application, value-to-thunk wrapping, adapter thunk force,
  and immediate `MakeThunk` bodies now avoid several continuation frames.
- Added an env-preserving proof table for runtime expressions, allowing catch
  bodies that cannot extend the caller environment to skip body-env cloning.
- Added known-state route proofs for direct `ref` get/set paths, keeping mutable
  `for` loop sums on the certified direct state path.
- Documented loop/effect microbenchmarks in the README. On the release binary,
  direct recursive 20x20 sum is the fastest baseline, while std `for` sum and
  nondeterministic `(each 1..20 + each 1..20).list` are now in the same order of
  magnitude through the effect runtime.
- Updated Japanese install guidance and Zed extension guidance to this release.
- Bumped the Zed extension package version to `0.0.11`.

## Install

Unix-like systems:

```sh
curl -fsSL https://yulang.momota.pw/install.sh | sh -s -- --version v0.1.0-alpha.6
```

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
Invoke-WebRequest https://yulang.momota.pw/install.ps1 -OutFile install.ps1
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\install.ps1 -Version v0.1.0-alpha.6
```

## Known Limitations

- The language and standard library remain alpha-stage and may still change.
- Local realm/band support is still limited to local editable and installed
  snapshots. Remote providers, lock-file UX, and version-family solving remain
  future work.
- The evidence VM is the default runtime path, but the control VM remains
  available as a fallback/debug path while runtime hardening continues.
- Direct recursion remains the fastest baseline. The current optimization work
  narrows the common effect-runtime overhead for std loops and nondeterministic
  code rather than making library effects as cheap as plain recursion.
